body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
July 6, 2026
Stablecoin market cap hits a record ~$322B, now exceeding the FX reserves of 95 countries, as issuers race to comply with the GENIUS Act's July 18 rulemaking deadline.
Stablecoin News
Stripe, Visa, BlackRock back new OUSD stablecoin to rival Tether and Circle

Open Standard, a coalition of 140+ businesses, launched Open USD (OUSD) on Solana, returning reserve yield to businesses instead of the issuer. Circle shares slipped 13% on the news; Tether's CEO called it 'Player 2.'
Revolut to delist USDT in Europe by August 31 over MiCA non-compliance
Tether has not sought MiCA licensing, forcing Revolut to drop USDT from EU listings while Circle's licensed USDC keeps its access, per MiCA rules effective July 1.
GENIUS Act stablecoin rules due July 18 as agencies race final rulemaking
OCC, FDIC, Treasury, FinCEN, OFAC and the Fed must finalize stablecoin issuer rules by the one-year statutory deadline; comment periods closed June 9 with the Fed's proposal still outstanding.
Circle expands USDC access via Standard Chartered, mints $250M on Solana
Circle enabled institutional USDC minting and redemption for Standard Chartered clients and minted an additional 250 million USDC on Solana this week.
Hacking & Security Incidents
H1 2026 crypto hacks hit record 207 incidents, $972M stolen
Despite more frequent attacks, total losses fell versus prior years; North Korea-linked groups accounted for roughly 66% ($643M) of all funds stolen in the first half of 2026.
Drift Protocol exploit remains the largest hack of 2026 at ~$285-295M
A Lazarus Group social-engineering campaign against Drift Protocol drained roughly $285-295 million, the single biggest crypto theft so far this year.
KelpDAO loses $293M in cross-chain bridge exploit
Attackers exploited a LayerZero-based bridge flaw to drain 116,500 rsETH from Kelp DAO, ranking among 2026's largest DeFi exploits.
Mystery attack drains hundreds of EVM wallets, root cause still unknown
On-chain investigator ZachXBT flagged an ongoing cross-chain wallet-draining campaign that has taken over $107K via suspected MetaMask phishing emails; no confirmed exploit vector yet.
